name: Comedy
description: Stories designed to amuse through humor, incongruity, and social friction, typically resolving tension through reconciliation or restored balance.

nodes.label: Comedy Promise
nodes.definition: The audience is promised amusement and emotional relief through humor, absurdity, or social friction, with tension resolving in reconciliation, restored balance, or joyful release rather than lasting harm.
exit_conditions: Core constraints are established that fulfill this promise
failure_modes: Attempts humor but doesn't amuse
Promises lightness but delivers mean-spiritedness
signals_in_text: Tone is inviting and playful from the opening
Characters and situations contain visible incongruities

nodes.label: Humor Must Be the Primary Engine
nodes.definition: The story must generate humor as its primary audience experience — through dialogue, situation, character behavior, or structural irony — not merely include occasional jokes in an otherwise non-comic story.
exit_conditions: Humor is present in the majority of scenes and drives engagement
failure_modes: Humor is incidental rather than structural
Story is serious with occasional jokes
signals_in_text: Most scenes contain at least one comic beat
Audience response is primarily amusement

nodes.label: Incongruity Creates Comic Tension
nodes.definition: Comedy requires visible incongruity — between what characters expect and what happens, between social roles and actual behavior, between self-image and reality.
exit_conditions: At least one major source of incongruity sustains the comedy
failure_modes: Everything is as expected — no surprise or mismatch
Incongruity is random rather than character-driven
signals_in_text: Gap between what characters believe and what audience knows
Social expectations subverted

nodes.label: Stakes Are Real but Recoverable
nodes.definition: Comedy needs genuine stakes to create tension, but those stakes must be ultimately recoverable — the audience trusts that lasting harm will not occur.
exit_conditions: Stakes are established that matter to characters but don't threaten permanent destruction
failure_modes: No stakes at all — nothing to lose
Stakes are so high they override the comic tone
signals_in_text: Characters face consequences that feel urgent in the moment
Audience senses a safety net even during tension

nodes.label: Resolution Trends Toward Reconciliation
nodes.definition: Comedy generally resolves toward reunion, reconciliation, or restored balance — the social order is repaired, relationships are mended, and the ending is more harmonious than the middle.
exit_conditions: Story ends with social harmony restored or improved
typical_variants: Status quo restored with added wisdom
failure_modes: Ending is bleak or punishing
Resolution undermines the comic tone
signals_in_text: Final scenes include reunion, celebration, or shared laughter
Conflicts that drove the comedy are resolved

nodes.label: Screwball Comedy
nodes.definition: Fast-paced comedy driven by verbal sparring, gender dynamics, and social-class friction, often featuring a central couple whose attraction manifests as conflict.
entry_conditions: Central dynamic involves quick-witted characters in social friction
exit_conditions: Wit and verbal combat are the primary comic engine
failure_modes: Verbal exchanges are slow or uninspired
Sparring becomes genuinely hostile
signals_in_text: Rapid-fire dialogue
Verbal one-upmanship
Physical comedy alongside wit

nodes.label: Farce
nodes.definition: Comedy built on escalating misunderstandings, mistaken identities, and physical chaos, where a small deception snowballs into increasingly absurd complications.
exit_conditions: Deception escalates through multiple layers before exposure
failure_modes: Complications don't escalate — chaos is static
Resolution is too easy — truth revealed without consequences
signals_in_text: Characters maintaining increasingly difficult deceptions
Near-misses and close calls
Physical comedy driven by panic

nodes.label: Dark Comedy
nodes.definition: Comedy that derives humor from subject matter typically considered serious, taboo, or disturbing — finding absurdity in mortality, crime, or social dysfunction.
entry_conditions: Subject matter includes dark or taboo elements
exit_conditions: Dark subject matter is treated with comic perspective without losing all emotional weight
failure_modes: Crosses into genuine cruelty without comic framing
Dark elements overwhelm the humor
signals_in_text: Juxtaposition of serious situations with absurd reactions
Characters treating terrible situations with inappropriate normalcy

nodes.label: Fish-Out-of-Water Comedy
nodes.definition: Comedy centered on a character placed in an environment where they don't belong — their unfamiliarity generates humor through cultural collision and failed adaptation.
entry_conditions: Protagonist is displaced from their normal context
exit_conditions: Character's displacement generates sustained comic friction
failure_modes: Character adapts too quickly — displacement vanishes
Humor relies on mockery rather than mutual discovery
signals_in_text: Character misreads social cues
Basic tasks become comic challenges
Gradual understanding between character and new environment

nodes.label: Social Setting Amplifies Friction
nodes.definition: Comedy settings should create situations where social friction is maximized — gatherings, workplaces, shared spaces — where characters cannot avoid each other or their problems.
exit_conditions: Setting forces characters into comic proximity
failure_modes: Characters can easily avoid each other
Setting has no social rules to subvert
signals_in_text: Characters thrown together by circumstance
Social expectations create pressure that generates humor

nodes.label: Social Rules Exist to Be Subverted
nodes.definition: Comedy requires visible social norms, expectations, or propriety that characters can violate — the humor comes from the gap between expected behavior and actual behavior.
exit_conditions: Social expectations are clear enough that their violation registers as comic
failure_modes: No visible norms to subvert
Norm violation is invisible to the audience
signals_in_text: Characters aware of what they 'should' do vs. what they actually do
Authority figures or social gatekeepers whose expectations are defied

nodes.label: Timing and Deadline Create Urgency
nodes.definition: Comedy often uses deadlines or timing mechanisms — the wedding is tomorrow, the boss arrives in an hour, the lie must be maintained until Saturday — to compress action and raise comic stakes.
exit_conditions: Time pressure forces characters to act rashly, increasing comic potential
typical_variants: Discovery deadline (secret must be maintained until X)
failure_modes: No time pressure — characters can solve problems at leisure
Deadline has no consequences if missed
signals_in_text: References to impending event
Characters racing against time
Pacing accelerates as deadline approaches

nodes.label: Comic Setup and Disruption Scene
nodes.definition: An early scene that establishes the normal order and then introduces the comic disruption — the lie, the displacement, the misunderstanding — that will drive the comedy.
exit_conditions: The source of comic friction is in motion
failure_modes: Setup is too long before the comedy begins
Disruption is too small to sustain a story
signals_in_text: Audience recognizes the gap between what characters believe and what's true
First major laugh is generated

nodes.label: Comic Escalation / Complication Scene
nodes.definition: A scene (or sequence) where the comic situation gets worse — the lie requires a bigger lie, the misunderstanding deepens, the fish-out-of-water faces a bigger challenge — before resolution is possible.
exit_conditions: Situation is more complicated and more absurd than before
failure_modes: Complications don't build on each other
Escalation is repetitive rather than progressive
signals_in_text: Each new complication makes the prior solution worse
Characters' stress increases while audience amusement increases

nodes.label: Comic Climax / Exposure Scene
nodes.definition: The scene of maximum comic chaos — all deceptions are exposed, all misunderstandings collide, or the absurdity reaches its peak — before the resolution restores order.
exit_conditions: Truth is out, chaos has peaked, and resolution can begin
typical_variants: All parties converge in one scene
Climactic set piece of physical comedy
failure_modes: Climax fizzles — truth emerges quietly
Chaos is resolved too easily
signals_in_text: Maximum number of characters in one scene
Multiple reveals happening simultaneously
Biggest laugh of the story

nodes.label: Playful and Inviting Tone
nodes.definition: Comedy requires a predominantly playful tone — the narrative voice signals that events, while sometimes stressful for characters, are ultimately safe for the audience to enjoy.
exit_conditions: Tone is maintained through language, pacing, and situation framing
failure_modes: Tone becomes mean-spirited or cruel
Playfulness disappears during serious moments without returning
signals_in_text: Narrative voice has lightness even in tense moments
Characters' suffering is temporary and acknowledged with empathy

nodes.label: Humor at Characters' Permanent Expense
nodes.definition: Comedy fails when humor comes from genuine, lasting harm to sympathetic characters — when the audience laughs at suffering that isn't recovered from.
entry_conditions: Humor is generated from character distress
exit_conditions: Distress is temporary and characters recover or are reconciled
failure_modes: This node IS the failure mode — its presence indicates a genre violation
signals_in_text: Audience feels guilty laughing
Character suffering persists into the resolution

edges.label: Promise requires humor as primary engine
edges.effects_on_stakes: Every conflict must serve the comedy
common_alternatives: Dramedy balances humor with serious emotional weight
anti_patterns: Humor is incidental to a non-comic story

edges.label: Promise requires incongruity
edges.effects_on_stakes: Tension comes from mismatch, not danger
edges.effects_on_character: Characters must have blind spots or mismatched contexts
common_alternatives: Incongruity may be verbal, situational, or physical
anti_patterns: Everything is as expected — no comic gap

edges.label: Promise requires recoverable stakes
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes must matter but not permanently destroy
edges.effects_on_character: Characters face real pressure but will be okay
common_alternatives: Dark comedy pushes this boundary while maintaining comic framing
anti_patterns: Permanent tragedy in a comedy — audience feels betrayed

edges.label: Promise requires positive resolution
edges.effects_on_stakes: Audience trusts the story will end well
edges.effects_on_character: Characters will find reconciliation or restored balance
common_alternatives: Dark comedy may end ambiguously but with comic perspective intact
anti_patterns: Bleak or punishing ending that betrays the comic contract

edges.label: Promise requires playful tone
edges.effects_on_stakes: Tone signals safety even during comic crises
edges.effects_on_character: Characters' struggles are framed with lightness
common_alternatives: Tone may be warm, sharp, or absurdist depending on subgenre
anti_patterns: Mean-spirited or grim tone sustained throughout

edges.label: Incongruity as verbal/social sparring
preconditions: Incongruity is between quick-witted characters in social friction
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes are romantic and social — who will win the verbal battle
edges.effects_on_character: Characters defined by their wit and social position

edges.label: Incongruity as escalating misunderstanding
preconditions: Incongruity is between appearance and reality, growing more extreme
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each layer of deception adds more to lose
edges.effects_on_character: Characters become increasingly desperate to maintain deceptions
anti_patterns: Misunderstanding is small and easily cleared up

edges.label: Incongruity between serious subject and comic treatment
preconditions: Incongruity is between dark subject matter and comic perspective
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes may include death or crime but comic framing is maintained
edges.effects_on_character: Characters respond to terrible situations with inappropriate normalcy or absurdity
anti_patterns: Dark material overwhelms the comedy — becomes straightforward drama

edges.label: Incongruity as cultural/contextual displacement
preconditions: Incongruity is between character and their environment
edges.effects_on_character: Character must navigate unfamiliar social terrain
anti_patterns: Character is merely clumsy — no genuine cultural collision

edges.label: Screwball requires social friction setting
edges.effects_on_stakes: Social setting provides audience and witnesses for sparring
edges.effects_on_character: Characters perform for each other and for society
anti_patterns: Characters spar in isolation with no social context

edges.label: Farce requires visible rules to subvert
edges.effects_on_stakes: Breaking rules carries social consequences that fuel the comedy
edges.effects_on_character: Characters desperately try to maintain appearances
anti_patterns: No norms exist to violate — chaos without contrast

edges.label: Farce requires timing pressure
edges.effects_on_stakes: Deadline makes deception increasingly difficult to maintain
edges.effects_on_character: Characters must act faster and more recklessly
anti_patterns: No time pressure — characters can untangle mess at leisure

edges.label: Fish-out-of-water needs unfamiliar social context
edges.effects_on_stakes: Unfamiliar social rules create constant comic friction
edges.effects_on_character: Character must learn rules by breaking them
anti_patterns: Character already understands the new environment

edges.label: Social setting generates comic disruption
preconditions: Social setting established with its rules and expectations
edges.effects_on_stakes: Disruption occurs within a social context that makes it consequential
edges.effects_on_character: Character's disruption affects their social standing
common_alternatives: Disruption may be deliberate (lie) or accidental (misunderstanding)
anti_patterns: Disruption occurs in social vacuum with no witnesses or consequences

edges.label: Breaking rules creates escalating complications
preconditions: Social rules established and initially violated
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each rule violation requires a bigger cover-up
edges.effects_on_character: Character sinks deeper into comic trouble
anti_patterns: Rule violation has no consequences — nothing escalates

edges.label: Deadline forces comic climax
edges.effects_on_stakes: All deceptions must resolve before or at the deadline
edges.effects_on_character: Characters can no longer maintain separate versions of reality
anti_patterns: Deadline arrives but nothing is resolved

edges.label: Escalation builds to comic explosion
edges.effects_on_stakes: Everything comes to a head simultaneously
edges.effects_on_character: Characters must face the truth and each other
anti_patterns: Complications resolve one by one without a climactic convergence

edges.label: Unrecoverable harm violates comedy
preconditions: Characters suffer lasting, unrecovered harm played for laughs
edges.effects_on_character: Character becomes a victim rather than a comic participant
anti_patterns: This edge IS the anti-pattern — it describes what to avoid
